summary: Adenine and guanine are purines with a double-ring structure, while cytosine and thymine are pyrimidines with a single-ring structure.
Adenine and cytosine are purines, while guanine and thymine are pyrimidines.
All four DNA bases have identical ring structures.
DNA polymerase synthesises new DNA in the 5' to 3' direction and requires a primer to begin synthesis.
DNA polymerase synthesises new DNA in the 3' to 5' direction.
DNA polymerase can begin synthesis de novo without any primer.
Helicase unwinds the DNA double helix, while topoisomerase relieves the torsional strain ahead of the replication fork.
Topoisomerase unwinds the double helix, while helicase relieves torsional strain.
Primase is responsible for unwinding the DNA double helix.
The lagging strand is synthesised discontinuously as short Okazaki fragments that are later joined by DNA ligase.
The lagging strand is synthesised continuously like the leading strand.
Okazaki fragments are found on the leading strand.
Uric acid is the final degradation product of purine metabolism in humans, and its accumulation causes gout.
Urea is the final product of purine degradation in humans.
Purine metabolism produces ammonia as its end product.
RNA polymerase reads the template (antisense) strand of DNA and synthesises mRNA complementary to it during transcription.
RNA polymerase reads the coding (sense) strand to make mRNA.
DNA polymerase is responsible for transcription of mRNA.
CRISPR-Cas9 is a genome editing tool derived from bacterial adaptive immune systems that target viral DNA.
CRISPR was developed entirely in the laboratory with no natural origin.
CRISPR is a diagnostic tool that cannot modify DNA sequences.
PCR (polymerase chain reaction) amplifies specific DNA sequences using thermal cycling with Taq polymerase, enabling molecular diagnosis.
PCR requires living cells to amplify DNA sequences.
PCR can only amplify RNA, not DNA.
